tool_args.label: Flatbush Ave Bus Priority

payload.description: Bus priority improvements on Flatbush Avenue focusing on Downtown Brooklyn/Northern Flatbush section. Three design options being studied: curbside bus lanes, offset bus lanes, and center-running bus lanes.

tool_args.label: Flatbush Ave is a critical transportation corridor

payload.claim_text: Flatbush Ave is a critical transportation corridor in Brooklyn with connections to major job centers, housing, entertainment, culture, shopping, and recreation
payload.subject: Flatbush Ave

tool_args.label: Flatbush Ave is a Vision Zero Priority Corridor

payload.claim_text: Flatbush Ave is a Vision Zero (VZ) Priority Corridor with 11 VZ Priority Intersections, is located within a VZ Priority Zone and intersects with 18 other VZ Priority Corridors
payload.subject: Flatbush Ave

tool_args.label: Curbside bus lanes can be blocked frequently

payload.claim_text: Curbside bus lanes can be blocked frequently due to parking/loading demand
payload.subject: curbside bus lanes

tool_args.label: Offset bus lanes less likely to be blocked

payload.claim_text: Offset bus lanes are less likely than curbside lanes to be blocked due to parking/loading demand
payload.subject: offset bus lanes

tool_args.label: Center-running bus lanes minimize conflicts

payload.claim_text: Center-running bus lanes minimize bus-vehicle conflicts as well as bus lane blockages. Near level boarding platforms and minimal bus-vehicle conflicts help create high quality transit experience for riders that can resemble train service.
payload.subject: center-running bus lanes
